title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Inject malicious scripts into trusted websites to steal sessions, deface sites, or redirect users.

lesson.summary: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) allows an attacker to execute malicious JavaScript in the victim's browser. This occurs when untrusted data is included in a web page without proper validation or escaping.
realWorld: Samy MySpace Worm (Infected 1M+ profiles in 20 hours)
TweetDeck XSS Worm (Forced retweets of malicious script)
British Airways Magecart (Stole 380k credit cards via XSS)
defenses: Use safe DOM APIs (textContent instead of innerHTML)
mistakes: Blacklisting specific tags instead of whitelisting or escaping
Reflecting input directly in script tags
tips: Use modern frameworks (React, Vue) that escape output by default.
Implement a strict CSP to block inline scripts.
Set HttpOnly flags on session cookies.

arena.attackDescription: An attacker is uploading a comment containing an obfuscated `<svg onload="steal()">` payload aiming to steal admin cookies.
